$comment: Every tool below maps 1:1 onto a function in supabase/schema.sql or an n8n
webhook in n8n-workflows/. If a tool exists here with no implementation
there, Sena will hallucinate a success on the phone to a real guest — so the
names and arguments are deliberately identical on both sides.
serverUrl is the n8n webhook base; each tool posts to serverUrl and n8n
routes on the tool name. Secrets are referenced, never inlined.
Latency budget: a guest starts to feel the pause at about 900ms. deepgram
nova-2 (~150ms) + claude (~400ms) + eleven turbo (~250ms) leaves headroom.
That is why the model is Sonnet and not a bigger, slower one — on a phone
call, a wrong-sounding pause costs more trust than a marginally better
name: Sena — AI Front Desk
$firstMessage_comment: AI disclosure inside the first ten seconds is non-negotiable (§0 Disclosure Policy). It is here, in the very first utterance, not left to the model to remember.
firstMessage: Good day, {{hotel_name}}. This is Sena — I'm an AI assistant, and I can book a room for you or answer any questions. Calls are recorded so we can help you if anything goes wrong. May I start with your name?

transcriber.$comment: multi so an Amharic-speaking caller is transcribed, not mangled into phonetic English. endpointing is tuned long: guests reading out an email address pause mid-string, and cutting them off means a wrong address and a lost booking.

model.$temperature_comment: Low. This agent quotes prices and policies. Creativity here is a defect.
model.$messages_comment: Content is voice-agent/system-prompt.md with {{...}} substituted per hotel by the inbound-call workflow. Kept in the .md file so it can be reviewed and diffed like prose, not buried in JSON.

function.name: log_call_intent
function.description: Record why the guest called, as soon as it is clear. Call once per call.

function.name: check_availability
function.description: Find rooms available for a date range. Returns room types with nightly rate, how many are free, and the total for the stay. Call only once you have read the dates back to the guest and they have confirmed them. Never invent a room or a rate that this did not return.

function.name: hold_room
function.description: Place a provisional hold on a room so no other caller can be sold it while you finish this call. MUST be called before you say the total out loud. May fail if the last room went during the conversation — if it does, tell the guest honestly and check availability again.

function.name: save_guest_details
function.description: Persist the guest's details against the held booking. Call this ONLY after the guest has confirmed the whole block of details read back to them a second time (the double-confirmation gate). If any field is unclear after two attempts, escalate instead of guessing.

needs_approval.description: true if they asked for early check-in or late check-out

double_confirmed.description: Must be true. Set it only after the guest confirmed the full block read back to them. Sending false will be rejected.

function.name: send_payment_link
function.description: Email the guest a secure Paystack payment link during the call. Never take card details by voice. Fails if you have not captured an email address — if it does, ask for one, read it back letter by letter, and save the details again.

function.name: check_payment_status
function.description: Ask whether the guest's payment has cleared yet. Poll this while they are paying. Only when it returns paid may you tell the guest the booking is confirmed.

function.name: send_confirmation_package
function.description: Email the guest their confirmation and a link to their single-use QR Guest ID card, and notify the owner. Call ONLY after check_payment_status returned paid.

function.name: lookup_booking
function.description: Find an existing booking by its reference or the guest's phone number, for a guest calling about a stay they already have.

function.name: cancel_booking
function.description: Cancel a booking the guest already has. Find it first with lookup_booking and read the reference back to them before you cancel — a cancellation cannot be undone. This NEVER issues a refund: read the hotel's cancellation policy back word for word, tell the guest the hotel will be in touch about any refund, and escalate if they press you on it.

reference.description: the booking reference, if that is all you have

reason.description: what the guest said, in their words

function.name: escalate_to_human
function.description: Hand the call to a person. Use for: an upset or threatening caller, a safety issue, 10+ rooms or a corporate rate, a second failed payment, suspected fraud, a demand outside policy, or a critical field you still cannot hear clearly after two attempts. Escalating is never a failure; guessing is.

summary.description: One or two sentences for the human picking up, so the guest does not repeat themselves.

function.name: end_call
function.description: End the call once the guest has nothing further.

voice.$comment: turbo for latency. Pick a warm, mid-pitch, gender-neutral voice that carries an African English accent well — a guest in Pretoria should not feel they have been routed to a call centre abroad. eleven_turbo_v2_5 also carries Amharic acceptably; re-test if the voice is changed.

server.$comment: Every tool call posts here. n8n verifies the secret, routes on tool name, and returns the result. The DB is never exposed to Vapi directly.

$recording_comment: Consent is stated in firstMessage (POPIA, CLAUDE.md §9). Recording without that sentence is unlawful — if the greeting is ever edited, this stays true or recording must be turned off.
$maxDuration_comment: 15 minutes. A booking call runs 4–6; anything at 15 has gone wrong and should be with a human anyway.
$backgroundSound_comment: A faint room tone. Total silence between turns reads as a dropped call and guests hang up.

analysisPlan.summaryPrompt: Summarise the call in two sentences for the hotel owner: what the guest wanted, and what happened.
analysisPlan.successEvaluationPrompt: Did the guest get what they called for — a booking completed, a question answered, or a clean handover to a human? Answer true or false.
